failure_top_details_explained.explanation: Downloaded bytes matched a SHA-256 on the project blocklist (typically Reddit/i.redd.it placeholder or CDN “missing image” payloads). The URL returned *something*, but it is treated as unusable for training.
The `requests` library raised after `raise_for_status()`—typically HTTP 4xx/5xx (removed post, paywall, forbidden, server error). The log stores the exception class name only, not the status line.

failure_detail_glossary.(empty): No text was stored in the log’s `detail` column (unexpected for normal runs).
failure_detail_glossary.ChunkedEncodingError: Transfer-Encoding/chunked stream ended inconsistently (server or intermediary closed the connection early).
failure_detail_glossary.ConnectTimeout: Timed out while trying to establish a TCP connection to the server.
failure_detail_glossary.ConnectionError: Network-level failure connecting to the host (DNS, refused connection, reset, proxy issues, offline client, or remote server not accepting the connection).
failure_detail_glossary.ContentDecodingError: Response declared a compression encoding but bytes could not be decoded.
failure_detail_glossary.HTTPError: The `requests` library raised after `raise_for_status()`—typically HTTP 4xx/5xx (removed post, paywall, forbidden, server error). The log stores the exception class name only, not the status line.
failure_detail_glossary.InvalidSchema: URL scheme is not supported for this request configuration.
failure_detail_glossary.InvalidURL: URL string was malformed or not usable as an HTTP URL.
failure_detail_glossary.MissingSchema: URL lacked `http://` or `https://` (or similar), so `requests` could not fetch it.
failure_detail_glossary.ReadTimeout: Connection opened, but the server did not send a complete response within the read timeout.
failure_detail_glossary.SSLError: TLS/SSL handshake or certificate validation failed between client and server.
failure_detail_glossary.Timeout: HTTP request exceeded the configured timeout (connect or read phase; see the `requests` `Timeout` hierarchy).
failure_detail_glossary.TooManyRedirects: Redirect loop or excessive redirects when following the image URL (`allow_redirects=True` in the fetcher).
failure_detail_glossary.pil_verify_failed: Bytes did not pass a strict PIL open+verify check—corrupt file, wrong content type (HTML/JSON masquerading as an image), or unsupported/broken image data.
failure_detail_glossary.reddit_placeholder_sha256: Downloaded bytes matched a SHA-256 on the project blocklist (typically Reddit/i.redd.it placeholder or CDN “missing image” payloads). The URL returned *something*, but it is treated as unusable for training.
failure_detail_glossary.too_small: HTTP response body was smaller than 512 bytes (`06_cohort_fetch_images.py`). Often an error page, empty payload, or truncated response rather than a real image.

label_binary_convention: 0 = real, 1 = fake (project consolidation; verify in fakenews.tsv build)
